1.result: Status-page persisted model preserves default-page compatibility
1.verification: `pnpm --filter @uptimer/worker exec vitest run --config vitest.config.ts test/status-pages-migration.test.ts test/status-pages-schema.test.ts` exits zero; `pnpm --filter @uptimer/worker migrate:local` exits zero; a local D1 query proves exactly one default page exists and its monitor links match the pre-migration `show_on_status_page = 1` set
test_scenarios: migration seeds the default slug deterministically
page/monitor links support shared monitors and page-local order/grouping
page incident/maintenance links are unique
existing public monitors retain visibility through default-page links
malformed or duplicate slugs fail schema validation
the old boolean is not used to decide post-migration page membership

execution_evidence.verification_result: pass: focused tests, DB typecheck, Worker lint, and staged temporary local D1 migration passed. With one public and one hidden pre-existing monitor, post-0014 counts were default_pages=1, default_page_monitors=1, legacy_public_monitors=1, missing_from_default=0, hidden_on_default=0.
execution_evidence.verification_command: pnpm --filter @uptimer/worker exec vitest run --config vitest.config.ts test/status-pages-migration.test.ts test/status-pages-schema.test.ts && pnpm --filter @uptimer/db typecheck && pnpm --filter @uptimer/worker lint && staged temporary D1 execution of migrations 0001-0014
execution_evidence.notes: QA rework resolved: the staged D1 migration test now seeds a public and hidden monitor before applying 0014, proving the default-page link set exactly preserves legacy public visibility. The scoped diff remains migration, matching Drizzle schema, and focused tests only.

2.result: Admin APIs manage explicit status-page publication links safely
2.verification: `pnpm --filter @uptimer/worker exec vitest run --config vitest.config.ts test/admin-status-pages.test.ts test/admin-incidents.test.ts test/admin-maintenance-windows.test.ts` exits zero
test_scenarios: admin CRUD validates normalized unique slug
monitor assignment replaces page links atomically and retains a shared monitor on another page
incident/maintenance create and patch require explicit non-empty `status_page_ids`
monitor links remain impact metadata
invalid resource IDs fail without partial links
deletion rejects linked pages and parent deletion removes its page links

execution_evidence.verification_result: pass: 6 focused suites / 10 tests plus DB and Worker typecheck/lint passed.
execution_evidence.verification_command: pnpm --filter @uptimer/worker exec vitest run --config vitest.config.ts test/status-pages-migration.test.ts test/status-pages-schema.test.ts test/admin-status-pages.test.ts test/admin-incidents.test.ts test/admin-maintenance-windows.test.ts test/admin-monitor-response-assertions.test.ts && pnpm --filter @uptimer/db typecheck && pnpm --filter @uptimer/worker typecheck && pnpm --filter @uptimer/worker lint
execution_evidence.notes: TDD RED evidence: admin status-page route test initially failed because the route did not exist. GREEN added only Admin CRUD, validated page/monitor links, explicit status_page_ids for incident/maintenance creation, maintenance link replacement, and restrictive deletion cleanup. YAGNI audit: public routes, snapshot refresh, frontend UI, custom domains, and tenant/RBAC were not changed.

3.result: Page-scoped public APIs enforce publication boundaries
3.verification: `pnpm --filter @uptimer/worker exec vitest run --config vitest.config.ts test/public-status-pages.test.ts test/public-status.test.ts test/public-homepage-downgrade-guard.test.ts` exits zero
test_scenarios: slug routes return only linked monitors with page-local group/order
explicit page incidents/maintenance render even with no monitor relation
linked monitor IDs describe impact only
page A cannot read page B monitor latency/uptime/outages/day-context, incident history, maintenance history, or analytics
unknown slug and outside resource return standard `NOT_FOUND`
default aliases preserve prior public endpoint output

execution_evidence.verification_command: pnpm --filter @uptimer/worker typecheck && pnpm --filter @uptimer/worker exec vitest run --config vitest.config.ts test/public-status-pages.test.ts test/public-status.test.ts test/public-homepage-downgrade-guard.test.ts && pnpm --filter @uptimer/worker lint && git diff --check
execution_evidence.notes: Planner rework removed U3 test-first posture because its historical RED phase cannot be reconstructed from the uncommitted working tree. Existing page-isolation regressions remain the automated verification contract.

4.result: Page-qualified Worker snapshot persistence prevents cross-page D1 or isolate-cache reads
4.verification: `pnpm --filter @uptimer/worker exec vitest run --config vitest.config.ts test/public-page-keys.test.ts test/snapshots-public-status.test.ts test/snapshots-public-homepage.test.ts test/snapshots-public-monitor-fragments.test.ts` exits zero
test_scenarios: centralized key factory creates distinct snapshot, artifact, fragment, runtime, and guard keys per page
D1 and Worker isolate-cache reads reject/ignore another page's rows
status/homepage artifacts and stale fallbacks remain within one page
default aliases use the default page identity

discovery_cache.reason: hard-coded status key and isolate cache
hard-coded homepage/artifact keys and isolate caches

execution_evidence.verification_result: pass: 68 focused tests, Worker typecheck, Worker lint, and git diff --check
execution_evidence.verification_command: pnpm --filter @uptimer/worker exec vitest run --config vitest.config.ts test/public-page-keys.test.ts test/snapshots-public-status.test.ts test/snapshots-public-homepage.test.ts test/snapshots-public-monitor-fragments.test.ts && pnpm --filter @uptimer/worker typecheck && pnpm --filter @uptimer/worker lint && git diff --check
execution_evidence.notes: After replan, U4 covers Worker snapshot persistence only. Non-default slug status reads its own page-qualified status key and falls back to page-scoped live compute on miss; it never reads the default snapshot. Page snapshot production remains U5; Pages/browser/query caches remain U6.

5.result: Bounded dirty-page snapshot refresh publishes page-qualified snapshots with continuation semantics
5.verification: `pnpm --filter @uptimer/worker exec vitest run --config vitest.config.ts test/internal-sharded-public-snapshot.test.ts test/internal-scheduled-check-batch.test.ts test/index-scheduled.test.ts test/internal-runtime-fragments-refresh.test.ts` exits zero
test_scenarios: a monitor runtime update marks only pages containing it dirty
page relation/incident/maintenance mutations mark only directly linked pages dirty
a bounded scheduled/internal batch publishes a continuation for remaining pages
shared monitors refresh every linked page exactly once
each non-default page writes only its own qualified snapshot and fragment keys
stale/missing page fragments do not publish another page's snapshot
default page retains existing scheduled behavior

parallel_probes.output: current continuation and bounded-batch call graph with affected-page insertion points
fragment/guard key lifecycle and invalidation map

execution_evidence.verification_result: pass: 41 U5 baseline tests, 13 page-refresh/admin tests, Worker typecheck, Worker lint, and git diff --check
execution_evidence.verification_command: pnpm --filter @uptimer/worker exec vitest run --config vitest.config.ts test/internal-sharded-public-snapshot.test.ts test/internal-scheduled-check-batch.test.ts test/index-scheduled.test.ts test/internal-runtime-fragments-refresh.test.ts && pnpm --filter @uptimer/worker typecheck && pnpm --filter @uptimer/worker lint && git diff --check
execution_evidence.notes: Added append-only status_page_refresh_queue migration. Admin monitor/incident/maintenance create/patch/delete and status-page create/update enqueue only affected page IDs. Scheduler consumes a bounded batch (5) per tick: compute page-scoped payload, write status:page:<id>, ack on success; failure retains the queue item. Page live-miss also writes its own qualified snapshot. Default page retains existing scheduled behavior.

6.result: Slug-routed status page UI has page-isolated caches
6.verification: `pnpm lint && pnpm typecheck && pnpm test && pnpm --filter @uptimer/worker migrate:local` exit zero; local browser verification creates two pages with a shared and exclusive monitor and confirms `/status/:slug` isolation; browser, Pages HTML cache, Worker hot cache, and React Query warm page A without serving it to page B; controlled Dev and Production Tail show `BAD_OR_GE10 count=0` for affected scheduled/internal/public paths; each page's `homepage`, `status`, and `homepage-artifact` parity endpoint returns `200`
test_scenarios: router extracts validated slug
status/history/detail UI retains slug in every request and query key
Pages injected HTML, Worker hot cache, browser localStorage/public cache, and React Query cache are page-qualified
browser reload/local cache does not reuse another page payload
admin UI manages pages and explicit page assignments
default `/` remains a compatibility redirect/render
missing slug shows a safe not-found state
no custom domain, tenant UI, or per-page notification controls are added

execution_evidence.verification_result: pass: pnpm lint + typecheck + test (491 tests, 58 files) + migrate:local all exit zero
execution_evidence.verification_command: pnpm lint && pnpm typecheck && pnpm test && pnpm --filter @uptimer/worker migrate:local
execution_evidence.notes: U6 frontend: router adds /status/:slug route; StatusPageSlugContext propagates slug to all public API calls, query keys, and localStorage keys; Pages worker HTML injection cache key includes slug; refreshQueuedStatusPageSnapshots catches errors to avoid blocking scheduled tick. HITL browser verification (two pages with shared/exclusive monitor, /status/:slug isolation, Dev/Prod Tail) still required by user.

plan_summary: Replace Uptimer's single implicit public status page with explicit status pages while preserving a default-page compatibility path and the released Free Plan snapshot CPU profile.
